retry_reason: A put operation cannot be retried; retrying would cause a double-put and potentially use-after-free.
contract_summary: This is a release (put) wrapper for BPF-allocated objects. It decrements the reference count of the object pointed to by kptr. If the reference count reaches zero, the object is freed. After calling bpf_obj_drop, the caller must not access the object. Each call must be matched with a prior acquisition (bpf_obj_new or bpf_refcount_acquire). The function always executes and requires no return value check.

judge.reason: The function bpf_obj_drop_impl is solely a put wrapper that decrements the reference count of a BPF-allocated object via kptr->refcount.refs. It has no additional logic beyond releasing the reference.
